englishTranslation: So verily, with the hardship, there is relief.
Then which of the Blessings of your Lord will you both deny?
And whosoever puts his trust in Allah, then He will suffice him.
O you who believe! Seek help in patience and As-Salat. Truly! Allah is with the patient ones.
Say: O My slaves who have transgressed against themselves! Despair not of the Mercy of Allah, verily Allah forgives all sins. Truly, He is Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful.
And I created not the jinns and humans except they should worship Me
O you who believe! Fear Allah, and die not except as Muslims.
O you who believe! Fear Allah. And let every person look to what he has sent forth for tomorrow, and fear Allah. Verily, Allah is All-Aware of what you do.
Did you think that We had created you in play, and that you would not be brought back to Us?
He Who created death and life that He may test you which of you is best in deed. And He is the All-Mighty, the Oft-Forgiving.
O mankind! Verily, the Promise of Allah is true. So let not this present life deceive you.
And when My servants ask you concerning Me, indeed I am near. I respond to the invocation of the supplicant when he calls upon Me.
Whosoever does good equal to the weight of an atom, shall see it. And whosoever does evil equal to the weight of an atom, shall see it.
If you are thankful, I will give you more, but if you are ungrateful, verily! My Punishment is indeed severe.
O mankind! Fear your Lord, and fear a Day when the father cannot avail his son in anything, nor can the son avail his father in anything.
The mutual rivalry (for piling up of worldly things) diverts you, until you visit the graves (till you die).
Verily, over you (are appointed angels) to watch you, honourable, writing down your deeds. They know all that you do.
Fear Allah, and know that Allah is the Best of those who keep watch.
So when they chose to deviate [from the truth], Allah caused their hearts to deviate [from right guidance]...
O you who believe! Ward off yourselves and your families against a Fire (Hell) whose fuel is people and stones, prepared for the disbelievers.
And as for him who feared the position of his Lord and prevented the soul from desires, then indeed, Jannah will be [his] refuge.
And if Allah willed, He could take away their hearing and their sight; for Allah has power over all things.
Say, 'Indeed, my prayer, my sacrifice, my living and my dying are for Allah, Lord of the worlds.'
Fear the Fire, whose fuel is people and stones, prepared for the disbelievers.
Nay! But on their hearts is the Rân (covering of sins and evil deeds) which they used to earn.
